title: [lora_l2.c:434-436] rx_callback cleared but rx_callback_user_data may have allocated resources
description:
Completeness: Resource leak category was not checked.

In lichen_lora_l2_init() (line 434-436), rx_callback and rx_callback_user_data are initialized to NULL. However, the API contract for user_data ownership is not documented.

If a caller passes heap-allocated user_data to set_rx_callback(), then:
- stop() at line 583-584 sets both to NULL
- deinit() at line 765-766 sets both to NULL

Neither function provides a mechanism for the caller to clean up their user_data before it's orphaned.

Severity is P4 because callers should track their own allocations, but the API documentation should clarify ownership.
close_reason: Fixed: added doc note in set_rx_callback that user_data is caller-owned, not freed by module
